articles.headline: Kane eyes Lewandowski record: Tracking Europe's major goal, assist records
articles.description: Harry Kane is chasing Robert Lewandowski's incredible Bundesliga goal tally, while Kylian Mbappé seeks Champions League history. Here's the latest in Europe's record goal and assist tallies.

images.name: Harry Kane [1296x729]
images.caption: Kane scored his 20th Bundesliga goal of the season today after just 16 games.
images.credit: Photo by Tom Weller/picture alliance via Getty Images

images.name: Manchester United's Bruno Fernandes [1296x729]
images.caption: Bruno Fernandes' assist tally took a sizeable leap after Ruben Amorim was sacked as Manchester United manager.

images.name: Lamine Yamal, Kylian Mbappe [1296x729]
images.caption: Neither Kylian Mbappé nor Lamine Yamal are likely to break Lionel Messi's single-season goal and assist marks in LaLiga.

images.name: Inter Milan's Lautaro Martinez [1296x729]
images.caption: Lautaro Martinez easily leads the Seire A scoring chart, but he won't get near the single-season record.

images.name: Mason Greenwood aug 2025 [1296x729]
images.caption: Mason Greenwood was made to share the Ligue 1 top scorer title last season.

images.name: Vinicus Junior, Kylian Mbappe [1296x729]
images.caption: Kylian Mbappé is within reach of Cristiano Ronaldo's single-season Champions League goal record.
images.credit: David S. Bustamante/Real Madrid via Getty Images

images.name: Bayern Munich holds on for 3-2 win
images.caption: Harry Kane scores a brace as Bayern Munich beat Eintracht Frankfurt 3-2.

articles.headline: Transfer rumors, news: Man United keeping tabs on Stuttgart's Andrés
articles.description: Manchester United could look to the Bundesliga for their newest midfield signing. Transfer Talk has the latest.

images.name: Chema Andrés [1296x729]
images.credit: (Photo by Harry Langer/picture alliance via Getty Images)

images.name: Is Lamine Yamal the best player in the world?
images.caption: The 'ESPN FC' crew react to Lamine Yamal becoming the youngest player to score a LaLiga hat-trick this century as Barcelona beat Villarreal 4-1.

articles.headline: Arsenal win ugly vs. Chelsea; Bayern close in on Bundesliga title; more
articles.description: Arsenal muscled past Chelsea on Sunday, but coach Mikel Arteta can't be happy at how difficult things look for his team. Also: Bayern Munich likely wrapped up the Bundesliga with a Der Klassiker win.

images.name: Arsenal celeb v Chelsea 260302 [1296x729]
images.credit: Stuart MacFarlane/Arsenal FC via Getty Images

images.name: Can Arsenal ride set-pieces all the way to the title?
images.caption: Shaka Hislop and Steve Nicol wonder if Arsenal are too reliant on set-pieces to win the Premier League title.

keyEvents.text: Substitution, FC Augsburg. Mads Pedersen replaces Dimitris Giannoulis because of an injury.
Second Half begins FC Bayern München 0, FC Augsburg 0.
Keven Schlotterbeck (FC Augsburg) is shown the yellow card for a bad foul.
Goal! FC Bayern München 1, FC Augsburg 0. Harry Kane (FC Bayern München) converts the penalty with a right footed shot to the top right corner.
Substitution, FC Augsburg. Mert Kömür replaces Arne Maier.
Substitution, FC Augsburg. Samuel Essende replaces Phillip Tietz.
Substitution, FC Bayern München. Serge Gnabry replaces Kingsley Coman.
Substitution, FC Bayern München. Konrad Laimer replaces Raphaël Guerreiro.
Substitution, FC Bayern München. Leroy Sané replaces Michael Olise.
Substitution, FC Augsburg. Henri Koudossou replaces Marius Wolf.
Substitution, FC Augsburg. Rubén Vargas replaces Alexis Claude-Maurice.
Substitution, FC Bayern München. Thomas Müller replaces Jamal Musiala.
Second yellow card to Keven Schlotterbeck (FC Augsburg) for a bad foul.
Goal! FC Bayern München 2, FC Augsburg 0. Harry Kane (FC Bayern München) converts the penalty with a right footed shot to the bottom left corner.
Substitution, FC Bayern München. Sacha Boey replaces Alphonso Davies.
Goal! FC Bayern München 3, FC Augsburg 0. Harry Kane (FC Bayern München) header from very close range to the bottom left corner. Assisted by Leon Goretzka with a through ball.
Second Half ends, FC Bayern München 3, FC Augsburg 0.

article.headline: Kane nets hat trick as Bayern beat Augsburg
article.description: Bayern Munich's Harry Kane scored a hat trick with two of the goals in second half stoppage time, to give the league leaders a 3-0 win over visitors Augsburg on Friday.
article.linkText: Kane nets hat trick as Bayern beat Augsburg

article.story:
Bayern Munich's Harry Kane scored a hat trick with two of the goals in second half stoppage time, to give the league leaders a 3-0 win over visitors Augsburg on Friday and open up an eight-point gap at the top with their sixth straight clean sheet across all competitions.

The Bavarians, who take on Paris St Germain in the Champions League on Tuesday, are undefeated in the Bundesliga and on 29 points following their fifth consecutive league game without conceding a goal.

Second-placed RB Leipzig, who travel to Hoffenheim on Saturday, are on 21 points.

As expected Bayern had more than 70% possession for much of the first half but Augsburg had keeper Nediljko Labrovic to thank for a goalless first half after the Croatia international stopped efforts from Harry Kane, Jamal Musiala and Leon Goretzka, who also hit the crossbar four minutes after the restart.

Labrovic then denied Michael Olise in the 51st but could do nothing to prevent Kane from scoring with a well-taken 63rd minute penally.

The England captain was then awarded another penalty following a lengthy VAR review in stoppage time with Augsburg's Keven Schlotterbeck sent off for tripping him in front of goal.

Labrovic was beaten by Kane after picking the wrong side again before the forward completed his hat-trick with a fine touch and header seconds later to take his league tally to 14 goals.
